title: coryphantha clavata subsp. stipitata
intro: Coryphantha clavata subsp. stipitata, also known as the Stipitata Cory Cactus, is a rare and lesser-known species of cactus native to Mexico, belonging to the Coryphantha genus. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Coryphantha clavata subsp. stipitata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antifungal activity, making it a potential treatment for a range of health issues. In herbal preparations, Coryphantha clavata subsp. stipitata is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to harness the plant's medicinal properties for therapeutic use.
